text.tspan: Case Study: Free H atom on Mo3Pd(111) Surface

tspan.tspan: Conclusion: Based on the performed calculations, the most stable configuration for a single free
hydrogen atom (H) on the Mo3Pd(111) surface is at a 3-fold Mo-Mo-Pd hollow site. The calculated
adsorption energy for this configuration is **-2.530 eV**. As this is the only configuration evaluated, it
represents the most stable site found in the current study. Further exploration of other potential sites
would be necessary to definitively confirm it as the global minimum.
History: Iteration 1 - [Hollow-3] Mo-Mo-Pd,
E_ads = -2.530 eV, perfect ads.
Reasoning: Continue Trying other high-
coordination sites. Bridge sites are often very
stable for H atom. Available bridge sites
include [Bridge] Mo-Mo & [Bridge] Mo-Pd. So,
I will try [Bridge] Mo-Pd this time.

text.tspan: typically more stable for atomic adsorbates.
Pd. So, I will try [Hollow-3] Mo-Mo-Pd this

tspan.tspan: Conclusion: Based on the provided computational results, current attempt placed the hydrogen atom at
a 2-fold bridge site between a Mo and a Pd. This configuration was unstable and the adsorbate atom
spontaneously slipped to the same hollow site found in the previous attempt, with a nearly identical
energy of **-2.526 eV**. So, the most stable configuration identified for a single free hydrogen atom
(H) on the Mo3Pd(111) surface is at a 3-fold Mo-Mo-Pd hollow site. The hydrogen atom is coordinated
to two Molybdenum (Mo) atoms and one Palladium (Pd) atom.
Observation: (1) Configuration of iter. 2
slipped to the same site as iter. 1; (2)
Energies are nearly identical (-2.530 eV
Conclusion:
Global Minimum Found

text.tspan: thermodynamic preference for the Mo-Mo-Pd Hollow
site as the stable confguration for a single hydrogen
atom on Mo3Pd(111) surface. The attempt to stabilize
the adsorbate at the Mo-Pd Bridge site resulted in a
spontaneous slip toward the 3-fold hollow position.

tspan.tspan: The resulting configurations are energetically

text.tspan: degenerate, indicating that the bridge site is
unstable and the system converges to a single
global minimum at the mixed-metal hollow site
